trigger: Delta report contains NODE_RUN_MISSING for an AI or IO node. Runtime is already published and materialized.
constraints: runtime_version must be 'current' unless a specific version is required
node_name must include the full @hive suffix
runtime arg must NOT include @hive suffix — just the runtime name
hive must match the @hive suffix in node_name exactly
do_not_repeat: do not add publish_runtime_package before run_node if runtime is already materialized — check delta report payload_ref
do not use the node_name as the runtime arg — they are different identifiers

trigger: Delta report contains RUNTIME_PUBLISH_AND_DISTRIBUTE. An approved artifact bundle is available.
constraints: source.kind must be 'inline_package' or 'bundle_upload' — match what the artifact bundle produced
sync_to is a list of hive IDs to distribute the runtime to after publishing
update_to is the hive that should activate the new version — usually the target worker hive
do_not_repeat: do not add run_node in the same plan if the delta only requires publishing — run_node is a separate delta operation
do not invent sync_to hives — use only hives listed in the delta operation notes or manifest

trigger: Delta report contains NODE_CONFIG_APPLY_RESTART. WF or SY node with config drift that requires a restart to take effect.
constraints: node_control_config_set MUST come before restart_node — config is applied first, then node restarts to pick it up
both steps must reference the same hive and node_name
config in node_control_config_set must be the delta config (not the full config)
do_not_repeat: do not skip node_control_config_set and only restart — this leaves config unchanged
do not add restart_node for AI.* or IO.* nodes — those use NODE_CONFIG_APPLY_HOT with no restart
do not include unchanged config fields — only the fields that changed
